name: E2E: the two-phase contract holds — a privileged-pod file that the write-time per-file enforcer blocks is the same file the audit-time whole-tree gate flags red, and a clean file passes both phases
command:
printf 'apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
spec:
  containers:
  - securityContext:
      privileged: true
' > pod.yaml && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/enforce-file.sh" --file pod.yaml --quiet >/dev/null 2>/dev/null; we=$?; mkdir -p t && cp pod.yaml t/pod.yaml && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/composite-audit.sh" --root t 2>a.txt; ae=$?; printf 'name: ok
' > clean.yaml && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/enforce-file.sh" --file clean.yaml --quiet >/dev/null 2>/dev/null; ce=$?; [ "$we" -eq 1 ] && [ "$ae" -eq 1 ] && grep -q 'status=red' a.txt && [ "$ce" -eq 0 ] && echo write-and-audit-agree 1>&2
